description_long: This is a weathered stone obelisk with a rectangular shaft rising from a solid, square base. The surface of the shaft is rough and uneven, showing signs of age and exposure, with some visible cracks and discoloration. The base is blocky and robust, slightly wider than the shaft, and appears to be made from the same stone material, with moss or lichen growth along the bottom edge. One face of the shaft features a faint, shallowly carved cross, suggesting a commemorative or religious function. The tip of the obelisk is flat and slightly beveled, lacking any ornate finial or capstone. There are no visible inscriptions or elaborate decorations, giving the monument a simple, utilitarian appearance.
description: A weathered stone obelisk with a rough, rectangular shaft and a solid square base, featuring a faint carved cross on one face. The structure is simple and unadorned, with visible aging and moss at the base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rough stone shaft and square base, with visible cracks and surface texture.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the shaft and the blocky base, with some discoloration near the bottom.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the faintly carved cross on the shaft and the clean lines of the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the plain, unadorned shaft and the weathered base.
attribute_ornamentation: A faint, shallowly carved cross is present on one face of the shaft; otherwise, none visible.
attribute_base_design: The base is a solid, square block of stone, slightly wider than the shaft, with a flat top and rough, weathered sides. There is some moss or lichen growth along the bottom edge, but no decorative features or steps.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is flat and slightly beveled, with no unique features or decorations.
